symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral symmetrical ocular burning and foreign-body (gritty) sensation with photophobia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Keratoconjunctivitis sicca is a defining feature of the exocrine flare; bilateral symmetry is characteristic.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute infective keratitis or corneal ulcer
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral symmetrical involvement without a focal unilateral defect argues against a single-eye sight-threatening lesion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe xerostomia with absent salivary pooling, preventing swallowing of solids

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Salivary gland hypofunction with near-absent flow is the oral limb of the exocrinopathy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical inflammatory arthralgia and mild swelling of the MCP joints and knees

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical small-joint inflammatory arthralgia is a common systemic feature of a Sjögren flare.
Objective joint swelling points to inflammatory disease rather than a purely subjective pain syndrome.
Disabling fatigue is a common but non-specific systemic feature of autoimmune flares.
Fatigue overlaps with fibromyalgia but does not discriminate between the two.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral, symmetrical, non-tender parotid fullness without erythema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral symmetrical glandular enlargement is characteristic of Sjögren salivary involvement.
Suppurative parotitis is typically unilateral, hot, tender and erythematous with purulent duct discharge — the opposite of this finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of a focal corneal opacity or purulent ocular discharge on slit-lamp examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute infective keratitis or corneal ulcer
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A slit-lamp with fluorescein is required to confirm the absence of a corneal ulcer/abrasion; it cannot be established via chat, hence the urgent optometry referral.
Absence of fever makes acute suppurative bacterial parotitis or systemic infection unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No sudden vision loss and no single disproportionately painful eye

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute infective keratitis or corneal ulcer
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of unilateral severe pain and of vision loss lowers the probability of a focal sight-threatening lesion but does not fully exclude it without slit-lamp.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inability to swallow solid food today with reduced intake while living alone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severity of xerostomia sufficient to prevent solid intake marks a severe flare and drives the nutrition/hydration safety-net.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Bilateral symmetrical dry eyes, xerostomia, symmetrical inflammatory arthralgia and symmetrical non-tender glandular fullness together constitute a high-probability autoimmune exocrine flare in a patient with known primary Sjögren syndrome.

The pertinent negatives — afebrile, no unilateral severe eye, no vision loss — collectively argue against the sight-threatening and suppurative mimics and support proportionate outpatient management with safety-netting rather than emergency escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Slit-lamp biomicroscopy with fluorescein staining (urgent optometry/ophthalmology)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The only way to detect or exclude a corneal ulcer/abrasion or exposure keratopathy; cannot be done via chat — arrange within 1–3 days, sooner if red-flag ocular features appear.
The diagnosis is already established; repeat serology is not needed acutely — GP/specialist may review as part of longitudinal care, not part of the acute telehealth workup.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Parotid ultrasound / blood cultures / septic screen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No features of acute bacterial parotitis or systemic sepsis (bilateral, symmetrical, non-tender, afebrile) — ordering these would be over-investigation.
